title: eremophila willsii subsp. integrifolia
intro: Eremophila willsii subsp. integrifolia, also known as the emu bush or integrifolia eremophila, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine and is known for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eremophila willsii subsp. integrifolia include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, although modern herbal preparations may vary.
